preview_text:
The model rates Bosnia and Herzegovina as the marginal favorite at 45% win probability, against Qatar's 24% chance. The gap reflects a modest edge for Bosnia and Herzegovina in the statistical forecast, though the underdog remains far from eliminated. A draw carries 31% likelihood and sits as a plausible outcome.

The width of the win-draw-loss spread indicates a competitive match with genuine uncertainty. Bosnia and Herzegovina enters as preferred but not dominant; Qatar's quarter-chance and the substantial draw probability suggest the fixture could easily resolve in multiple directions. No outcome commands overwhelming confidence from the model.

The sportsbook consensus tilts notably more bullish on Bosnia and Herzegovina, assigning 54% to their win versus the model's 45%—a 9 percentage-point gap. Both sources agree a draw is secondary, though the market values it slightly lower. The divergence suggests bookmakers see a clearer Bosnia and Herzegovina advantage than the statistical model does. This dashboard makes no claim about which reading is more accurate.
